name: new-error-type-once
description: New error 'embedding dimension mismatch' in retrieval. 1 trace at 14:32. Agent recovered via keyword fallback. Never seen before.

item.title: New error type in customer-support-bot retrieval
item.description: Automated alert: previously unseen error type 'embedding dimension mismatch' detected on customer-support-bot.
item.note:
Investigated the new error type. Findings:

- Single occurrence: trace new-4401 at 14:32:08 UTC
- Error on span retrieval.vector_search: "embedding dimension mismatch: expected 1536, got 3072"
- This suggests the query was embedded with a different model (text-embedding-3-large = 3072 dims) than the index expects (text-embedding-ada-002 = 1536 dims)
- The agent recovered: after the vector search failed, it fell back to keyword-based search (span retrieval.keyword_fallback, 340ms)
- The trace completed successfully — end user got a response, albeit slightly lower quality (keyword vs semantic)
- Trace latency: 4.1s (slightly elevated due to the failed attempt + fallback, vs normal 2.8s p50)

Scope check:
- This is the FIRST time this error has appeared — searched all error messages in ClickHouse for "dimension mismatch": 0 prior occurrences
- 82 other traces in the same hour had no issues with the vector search span
- The embedding model in the successful traces: text-embedding-ada-002 (correct)
- The one failing trace also shows text-embedding-ada-002 in the config, but the actual API call returned 3072 dims
- Possible cause: transient API routing issue on OpenAI's side, or a race condition in model version selection

Since the agent recovered and this is a single occurrence of a never-before-seen error, it may be transient. But the dimension mismatch could indicate a configuration drift that hasn't fully manifested yet.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
